queries.name: About: Iceberg catalog explorer (DBA)
queries.description: Generated by examples/build-iceberg-dashboards.mjs — edit that script, not this entry.

cfg.content:
## Iceberg catalog explorer — DBA

Maintenance signals for every installed catalog, from Iceberg metadata only:
snapshot-expiry backlog, manifest fragmentation, metadata.json bloat,
merge-on-read delete manifests, and two **log panels** — the snapshot commit
timeline and the metadata-version timeline (`from` accepts relative times
like `-30d`).

The shared `catalog` filter narrows every tile; blank = all catalogs.
Detail tables (schema, partition spec, manifest inventory, full snapshot log)
live in the Library below the dashboard tiles — they are deliberately not on
the dashboard. Per-table small-file/skew/Parquet analysis: generate the
drill-down Library from `iceberg-install.json` (entry i5).

queries.name: Snapshot-expiry candidates
queries.sql:
SELECT concat(catalog, '.', ice_table) AS table, count() - 1 AS old_snapshots
FROM ice_meta_all.snapshots
WHERE 1 /*[ AND catalog = {catalog:String} ]*/
GROUP BY catalog, ice_table
HAVING old_snapshots > 0
ORDER BY old_snapshots DESC
LIMIT 25
queries.description: Snapshots per table beyond the current one — each is history that expireSnapshots() could reclaim. Big counts = expiry never runs.

queries.name: Manifest fragmentation (current snapshot)
queries.sql:
SELECT concat(ml.catalog, '.', ml.ice_table) AS table,
       count() AS manifests,
       round(sum(ml.added_files_count + ml.existing_files_count) / count(), 1) AS avg_files_per_manifest
FROM ice_meta_all.manifest_lists AS ml
INNER JOIN ice_meta_all.tables AS t
  ON ml.catalog = t.catalog AND ml.ice_table = t.ice_table AND ml.list_snapshot_id = t.current_snapshot_id
WHERE 1 /*[ AND ml.catalog = {catalog:String} ]*/
GROUP BY table
ORDER BY manifests DESC
queries.description: Manifests referenced by each table's CURRENT manifest-list, and how few files each tracks on average. Many manifests tracking few files each = rewriteManifests() candidate.

queries.name: Merge-on-read delete manifests
queries.sql:
SELECT concat(catalog, '.', ice_table) AS table, countIf(content = 1) AS delete_manifests
FROM ice_meta_all.manifest_lists
WHERE 1 /*[ AND catalog = {catalog:String} ]*/
GROUP BY catalog, ice_table
ORDER BY delete_manifests DESC
queries.description: Delete manifests (content=1) in each table's manifest-lists. A growing count means MOR deletes are piling up and reads pay for it — schedule rewrite_data_files/compaction.

queries.name: metadata.json versions on S3
queries.sql:
SELECT concat(o.catalog, '.', o.ice_table) AS table,
       countIf(o.kind = 'metadata.json') AS metadata_files_on_s3,
       max(t.num_snapshots) AS snapshots_in_current_metadata
FROM ice_meta_all.objects AS o
LEFT JOIN ice_meta_all.tables AS t ON o.catalog = t.catalog AND o.ice_table = t.ice_table
WHERE o.kind = 'metadata.json' /*[ AND o.catalog = {catalog:String} ]*/
GROUP BY table
ORDER BY metadata_files_on_s3 DESC
queries.description: Physical metadata.json objects per table vs. the versions the current metadata-log still references. A wide gap = metadata bloat (write.metadata.delete-after-commit.enabled is off).

queries.name: Physical objects per table
queries.sql:
SELECT concat(catalog, '.', ice_table) AS table,
       countIf(kind = 'data.parquet') AS data_files,
       countIf(kind = 'manifest') AS manifests,
       countIf(kind = 'metadata.json') AS metadata_versions
FROM ice_meta_all.objects
WHERE 1 /*[ AND catalog = {catalog:String} ]*/
GROUP BY catalog, ice_table
ORDER BY data_files DESC
queries.description: S3 object census per table by Iceberg role — the denominator for orphan hunting. Compare data.parquet counts against data_files() from the per-catalog drill-down to find unreferenced files.

queries.name: Snapshot commit timeline
queries.sql:
SELECT committed_at AS ts,
       multiIf(operation IN ('overwrite', 'delete'), 'warn', 'info') AS level,
       concat(catalog, '.', ice_table, ' — ', operation,
              ' (+', toString(added_records), ' rows, total ',
              formatReadableSize(total_files_size), ', snapshot ', snapshot_id, ')') AS msg
FROM ice_meta_all.snapshots
WHERE committed_at > {from:DateTime}
/*[ AND committed_at <= {to:DateTime} ]*/
/*[ AND catalog = {catalog:String} ]*/
ORDER BY ts DESC
LIMIT 500
queries.description: Every snapshot commit as a log line: operation, rows added, running total size. `from` is required and takes relative times (-30d, now-6M); operation overwrite/delete shows as warn.

queries.name: Metadata version timeline
queries.description: When each table's metadata.json was replaced (the Iceberg metadata-log). `from` is required and takes relative times (-30d).

queries.name: Detail: tables & properties
queries.sql: SELECT * FROM ice_meta_all.tables WHERE 1 /*[ AND catalog = {catalog:String} ]*/ ORDER BY catalog, ice_table
queries.description: One row per Iceberg table — current metadata file, snapshot, schema/snapshot counts, raw table properties JSON.

queries.name: Detail: full snapshot log
queries.sql: SELECT * FROM ice_meta_all.snapshots WHERE 1 /*[ AND catalog = {catalog:String} ]*/ ORDER BY catalog, ice_table, sequence_number
queries.description: Every snapshot of every table with its summary JSON.

queries.name: Detail: schema columns
queries.sql: SELECT * FROM ice_meta_all.schema_columns WHERE 1 /*[ AND catalog = {catalog:String} ]*/ ORDER BY catalog, ice_table, field_id
queries.description: Current schema of every table, one row per column.

queries.name: Detail: partition specs
queries.sql: SELECT * FROM ice_meta_all.partition_spec WHERE 1 /*[ AND catalog = {catalog:String} ]*/ ORDER BY catalog, ice_table, field_id
queries.description: Default partition spec of every table, one row per partition field. Empty = unpartitioned tables.

queries.name: Detail: manifest inventory
queries.sql: SELECT * FROM ice_meta_all.manifest_lists WHERE 1 /*[ AND catalog = {catalog:String} ]*/ ORDER BY catalog, ice_table, sequence_number
queries.description: Every manifest referenced by every manifest-list (all snapshots, not just current).
